description: A structured AI Product Leader learning book for understanding modern AI systems, prompting, token economics, RAG, agents, MCP, production AI infrastructure, and applied AI product strategy.
sourceNote: Generated from AI_Product_Leader_Syllabus.xlsx Master Syllabus tab. Modules 01–02 Active with live chapters; Modules 03–09 marked Coming Soon.

modules.title: Foundations of Modern AI Systems
modules.pmFocus: Every token costs money. Understanding tokenization, context windows, and model tiers lets you set product constraints, write better specs, and own AI feature unit economics.
modules.deliverable: Model selection memo: use case, quality reqs, cost estimate at scale, chosen model + rationale, risks
learningOutcomes: Explain context windows to stakeholders
Estimate monthly inference cost for a feature
Choose the right model tier for a use case
Have a credible LLM conversation with engineers

chapters.title: Understanding Attention Intuition from "The Illustrated Transformer"
From Transformers to LLMs — The PM Version
Tokens and Context Windows — The PM Version
AI Safety, RLHF, and Constitutional AI — The PM Version
InstructGPT and RLHF — The PM Version
Fine-Tuning vs Prompting — The PM Version
Temperature, Top-p, and Sampling — The PM Version
Why LLMs Hallucinate — The PM Version
Long Context Window Tradeoffs — The PM Version
Pre-training vs Fine-tuning vs RLHF — The PM Version
Hallucinations, Knowledge Cutoffs, and Model Limitations — The PM Version

modules.title: Model Landscape, Capabilities & Economics
modules.subtitle: Multimodal, model tiers, benchmarks, cost modelling
modules.pmFocus: Compare models like infrastructure choices, not brand fandom. Your eval set and unit economics matter more than leaderboard rank.
modules.deliverable: One-page model selection memo: use case, quality, latency, cost, models compared, rationale, risks, monitoring
learningOutcomes: Compare model families by product criteria
Interpret benchmarks without over-trusting leaderboards
Model monthly AI cost for a feature
Write a credible model selection memo
resources: Provider model cards and pricing pages

chapters.title: Model Families and the Competitive Landscape — The PM Version
Choosing Between GPT, Claude, Gemini, and Llama — The PM Version
Multimodal AI: Vision, Audio, Code, and Documents — The PM Version
Benchmarks: What PMs Should and Shouldn't Trust — The PM Version
Cost per Token, Latency, and Model Tier Tradeoffs — The PM Version
The Model Selection Memo — The PM Version

modules.title: Prompt Engineering — Techniques & Structure
modules.pmFocus: Prompt engineering is the new acceptance criteria. A well-structured prompt defines 'done' for the AI as clearly as a user story does for an engineer. Own this skill.
modules.deliverable: 3 production-quality system prompts for features in your product + an eval rubric for each
learningOutcomes: Write production-grade system prompts
Design CoT prompts for complex reasoning tasks
Use XML tags for reliable structured output
Version and A/B test prompts

modules.title: Token-Efficient Prompting
coreTopics: Structured JSON/XML to reduce explanation tokens
Efficiency metrics (cost per successful completion, token efficiency ratio)
modules.pmFocus: At 1M calls/day, a 3x reduction in prompt tokens can be the difference between a profitable and unprofitable AI feature. Token efficiency is a product economics lever — own the number in your unit economics doc.
modules.deliverable: Token efficiency audit: take 3 existing prompts from your product, compress each by ≥50%, validate quality parity, document savings at projected scale
learningOutcomes: Audit & compress a production prompt
Design a cache-friendly system prompt architecture
Define token budget in a PRD
Model AI feature unit economics with caching and batching

modules.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
modules.pmFocus: RAG is the most common production pattern for grounding LLMs in company knowledge. Retrieval precision directly reduces context tokens — a well-tuned retriever cuts input size 40–60% vs naive retrieval.
modules.deliverable: RAG pipeline spec for a real feature: chunking strategy, embedding model choice, vector DB selection, retrieval approach, eval plan, freshness strategy
learningOutcomes: Spec a RAG pipeline for a product feature
Diagnose poor retrieval quality
Choose a vector DB for a given use case
Design a RAG eval suite using RAGAS

modules.title: AI Agents & Agentic Systems
coreTopics: Web search / code interpreter / browser tools
modules.pmFocus: Agents introduce compounding failure modes and latency. Your job is defining the right HITL boundaries and what 'done' looks like for an agent task. Agent loops multiply token costs — efficient scratchpads are critical.
modules.deliverable: Agentic feature PRD: agent scope, tool list, HITL checkpoints, blast radius controls, failure handling, observability requirements, token cost estimate per agent run
learningOutcomes: Define agent scope & guardrails in a PRD
Design human-in-the-loop checkpoints
Choose the right agent topology
Identify and mitigate prompt injection risks
Estimate token cost for multi-step agent runs

modules.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
coreTopics: What MCP is & why it exists
MCP vs function calling vs plugins
Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code integrations
Trust levels: local vs remote servers
MCP as a platform distribution play
When to build an MCP server for your product
Competitive landscape (MCP vs A2A vs others)
modules.pmFocus: MCP is becoming the USB-C of AI tool connectivity. Understand when to expose your product via MCP and how that creates distribution in the agent ecosystem. This is a platform strategy question.
modules.deliverable: MCP build-vs-skip decision memo: should your product expose an MCP server? Who benefits? What tools would you expose? What are the security requirements?
learningOutcomes: Evaluate whether to build an MCP server for your product
Spec MCP security & trust requirements
Understand the MCP platform ecosystem
Map competitive implications

modules.title: AI Infrastructure & the Production Stack
modules.pmFocus: Observability tools let you see token spend by feature, by user segment, and by prompt version — this is your cost-of-goods dashboard for AI. Knowing this stack makes you a better partner to ML and platform engineers.
modules.deliverable: AI observability spec: what metrics to track, what dashboards to build, what alerts to set, and a cost-per-feature breakdown for your AI product
learningOutcomes: Define observability & monitoring requirements
Write guardrail policy specs
Build an AI A/B testing plan
Set up cost attribution by feature
Specify fine-tuning decision criteria
resources: 'Fine-tuning vs RAG vs prompting' decision guide

modules.title: AI Product Strategy & Applied Practice
coreTopics: Build vs buy vs API decisions
modules.pmFocus: The capstone synthesizes everything. Pick a real problem you are solving at work and design the full AI system — including token budget, caching strategy, eval plan, guardrails, and the product decisions only you can make.
modules.deliverable: Capstone: full agentic feature design — PRD with AI-specific sections, token budget, eval strategy, guardrail spec, HITL design, model selection rationale, and unit economics model
learningOutcomes: Lead AI product discovery end-to-end
Write a complete agentic feature PRD
Navigate AI ethics & compliance tradeoffs
Present AI architecture decisions to leadership
Define and own AI product metrics
